techniques.evidence_quote: a company working with our client organization was compromised. This third party had over-permissioned access which meant that misuse of the account allowed for broader access than intended or needed.
the federated user account was leveraged by the threat actor in order to move to the other environment and start leveraging access.
They affected changing the domain name system to redirect the client organization's website and email.

attack_chain.description: A third-party company with federated access to the client's AWS environment was compromised by the threat actor, providing an initial foothold via the trusted partner relationship.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a company working with our client organization was compromised. This third party had over-permissioned access which meant that misuse of the account allowed for broader access than intended or needed.

attack_chain.description: The threat actor leveraged the compromised third party's over-permissioned federated user account to authenticate into the client's AWS environment and gain broader access than intended.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the federated user account was leveraged by the threat actor in order to move to the other environment and start leveraging access.

attack_chain.description: Using the federated account's excessive permissions, the attacker modified DNS records to redirect the client organization's website and email traffic.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: They affected changing the domain name system to redirect the client organization's website and email.

initial_access_vector: Third-party company with over-permissioned federated access to the client's AWS environment was compromised, allowing the threat actor to pivot into the client environment.
privilege_escalation: Over-permissioned federated IAM account used by the third party provided broader access than needed, effectively granting the attacker elevated privileges without explicit escalation.
